Classify the following text:
EBIT EBIT is used as the measure of operating profit for the Group, the Industrial Business and its segments, as well as for Finan- cial Services.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
As earnings before interest and income taxes, EBIT refects the proft responsibility of the segments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The respective EBIT is derived from gross proft, which is the result of revenue less cost of sales.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Operating proft is then calculated, with consideration of selling expenses, general administrative expenses, research and non-capitalized devel- opment expenditures, and other income and expenses.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The EBIT of the Industrial Business additionally includes other rec- onciliation items of the Group that cannot be allocated to the segments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The EBIT for Financial Services already includes the interest result from operating activities, which is typical for the banking business.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
To provide a more transparent picture of our ongoing business, we also calculate and report adjusted EBIT for the Group, the Industrial Business and its segments and Financial Services.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The adjustments include individual items if they lead to mate- rial efects in a reporting year.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
These individual items may in particular relate to legal proceedings and related measures, restructuring measures and M&A transactions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Return on sales (Industrial Business) The return on sales is used to assess sales-based proftability in the Industrial Business.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
It is calculated as the ratio of EBIT to revenue.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Revenues are primarily infuenced by unit sales.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Based on the adjusted EBIT, we also report the adjusted return on sales for the Industrial Business and its segments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Free cash flow (Industrial Business) The main indicator of the fnancial strength of the Daimler Truck Group is the free cash fow of the Industrial Business.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In addition to cash fows from operating activities, this also includes cash fows from investing activities of the Industrial Business.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The cash fows from the purchase and sale of mar- ketable debt securities and similar cash investments included in cash fows from investing activities are eliminated, as these securities are classifed as cash and cash equivalents and their change is therefore not part of the free cash fow of the Indus- trial Business.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
On the other hand, efects in connection with the recognition of rights of use resulting from lessee accounting, which are mainly non-cash-efective, are included in the free cash fow of the Industrial Business.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Classify the following text:
Financial management at the Daimler Truck Group comprises capital structure management, cash and liquidity management, the management of market price risks (currencies, interest rates, commodity prices) as well as the management of pen- sion assets and credit-default and country risks.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Global fnan- cial management was carried out uniformly by the Treasury organization of the Daimler Truck Group within the framework of the legal provisions for all Group companies.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
It fundamen- tally operates within a predefned framework of guidelines, lim- its and benchmarks; organizationally, it is separate at the oper- ational level from the settlement, fnancial control, reporting and accounting functions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Capital structure management arranges the capital struc- ture of the Group and its subsidiaries.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The capital resources of the fnancial services, production, sales or fnancing compa- nies of Daimler Truck are allocated in accordance with the prin- ciples of cost- and risk-optimized fnancial and capital resources.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Liquidity management is intended to ensure that the Group is always able to meet its payment obligations.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
To this end, the Group records the cash fows from operating activities and from fnancial transactions in a rolling planning.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financing requirements are met by means of suitable liquidity manage- ment instruments (for example bank loans, bonds); the Group invests surplus liquidity in the money and/or capital market, taking expected returns and risks into account.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Factoring is frequently used to a limited extent to manage liquidity.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Our goal is to provide the liquidity that is deemed to be neces- sary at the best possible cost.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In addition to operating liquidity, the Daimler Truck Group has further liquidity reserves that are available at short notice.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The components of these additional resources are receivables from the fnancial services business that can be securitized on the capital market, a contractually committed syndicated credit facility (revolving credit facility), and an additional revolving credit facility agreed in 2022.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Cash management determines cash requirements and sur- pluses on a centralized basis.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In this context, liquidity is con- centrated centrally in bank accounts of the Daimler Truck Group via cash pooling procedures in various currencies.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Most payments between Group companies are made via internal clearing accounts, so that the number of external payment fows is kept to a minimum.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group has established standardized processes and systems to manage its bank and internal clearing accounts and to carry out auto- mated payment transactions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Market price risk management aims to limit the impact of fuctuations in currency exchange rates, interest rates and commodity prices on the earnings of the segments and the Group.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
To this end, the Group-wide risk volume (exposure) for these market price risks is frst determined.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For currencies, net exposure constitutes the relevant risk volume, which is reduced by netting foreign currency items among the Group companies.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Group then makes its hedging decisions on this basis.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This includes the volume to be hedged, the period to be hedged, and the choice of hedging instruments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The hedging strategy is defned at Group level and implemented uniformly.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Decision-making bodies are internal committees for market price risks that meet regularly.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Air pollution control GRI 305-6 As with other environmental aspects, our objective is to reduce the environmental impact of air emissions from production as much as possible.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
For Daimler Truck, the relevant air pollut- ants are nitrous oxides, sulfur oxides, carbon monoxide and particulate matter, which are mainly produced in combustion processes, as well as VOC emissions.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
Volatile organic com- pounds (VOC emissions) mainly arise in painting processes.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
Daimler Truck attaches particular importance to reducing VOC emissions.

This process has been intro- duced in Worth, the largest Mercedes-Benz truck assembly plant in the world.

Classify the following text:
Against this background, Regulation (EU) 2020/852 of the European Parlia- ment and of the Council of June18, 2020 on the establishment of a framework to facilitate sustainable investments, and amending EU Regulation (EU) 2019/2088 (Taxonomy Regula- tion) entered into force in 2020.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Taxonomy Regulation is a uniform and legally binding classifcation system stating which economic activities in the EU are considered taxonomy-aligned and thus environmentally sustainable with regard to six envi- ronmental objectives specifed by the Regulation.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The following environmental objectives are defned by the Taxonomy Regula- tion: - Environmental objective 3 Sustainable use and protection of water and marine resources - Environmental objective 6 Protection of biodiversity and ecosystems Due to the relief provision exercised in the last reporting year and thus the non-availability of prior-year fgures from the 2021 reporting year, no prior years values are presented in the cur- rent reporting year.

As a result, no qualitative information is included to describe the changes.

Classify the following text:
Taxonomy-eligible economic activities With the descriptions of economic activities in the delegated acts, the Taxonomy Regulation specifes which activities are generally taxonomy-eligible.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
As the determination of taxonomy eligibility for an economic activity is based on its output, it fol- lows that all activities, as well as the associated capital expen- diture and operating expenditure, that serve the production or leasing/fnancing of vehicles are also taxonomy-eligible.1 Accordingly, Daimler Truck does not carry out any independent activities in the context of Delegated Act (EU) 2022/1214 of March 9, 2022, which contains specifc regulations for the energy sector.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Activities in connection with this Delegated Act were analyzed.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
They serve the manufacturing activity due to their own use and are considered as immaterial for Daimler Truck.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Therefore, the corresponding information is not included.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Daimler Truck has classifed the following activities as taxonomy-eligible in accordance with the Taxonomy Regula- tion.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Under Activity 3.3, Daimler Truck includes all trucks and buses produced by Daimler Truck, independent of their CO2 emis- sions, as taxonomy-eligible, i.e., also all vehicles with combus- tion engines.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
The background to this is that the Taxonomy Reg- ulation does not contain any defnition of the term low carbon outside the technical screening criteria.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
Therefore, all vehicles can in principle be classifed as eligible and the term low car- bon is only to be applied to the assessment of taxonomy alignment within the framework of the technical screening cri- teria.2 Activities 6.3, 6.5 and 6.6 include leasing/fnancing activities relating to buses, cars and trucks, regardless of whether they are own or external brands.

Classify the following text:
Daimler Truck calculates and documents its CO2 emissions according to the Corporate Accounting and Reporting Stan- dard 2004 of the Greenhouse Gas Protocol Initiative, in accor- dance with the categories Scope1 and Scope 2.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
Scope1 and Scope 2 emissions are reported in accordance with the Con- trol approach of the Greenhouse Gas Protocol.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
Documented are all direct CO2 emissions from our Companys own sources (Scope1), indirect emissions resulting from the generation of the purchased electricity and district heat (Scope 2).
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
Scope1: We calculate our direct emissions from the combus- tion of fuels, heating oil, natural gas, liquefed petroleum gas, and coal with fxed CO2 emission factors as specifed by the World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD) or the German Emissions Trading Ofce (DEHSt), the regulation on Emission Reporting (BEV), the Global Emis- sions Model of Integrated Systems 4 (Gemis 4) as well as the Department of Energy Information Administration (DOE EIA).
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
Scope 2: We calculate the indirect emissions of district heating and electricity from external generation in a diferentiated manner in terms of time and region.
Answer: Environmental

Classify the following text:
CO2 accounting is carried out using the market-based accounting approach.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

In the context of the separation of the commercial vehicle business from the Mercedes-Benz Group in the 2021 fnancial year, Mercedes-Benz Group AG (formerly Daimler AG), Mercedes-Benz Verwaltungsgesellschaf fur Grundbesitz MBH (formerly Daimler Verwaltungsgesellschaf fur Grundbesitz MBH), and Daimler Truck Holding AG entered into a so-called deconsolidation agreement on August 6, 2021, as an annex to the spin-of and hive-down agreement, which took efect upon entry of the spin-of and hive-down in the commercial register of Mercedes-Benz Group AG as transferring legal entity on December 9, 2021.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This is intended to ensure that a de-facto majority of Mercedes-Benz Group AG at the General Meeting of Daimler Truck Holding AG does not lead to a control rela- tionship and a related full consolidation obligation of Daimler Truck Holding AG at Mercedes-Benz Group AG.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, the agreement provides that Mercedes-Benz Group AG and Mercedes-Benz Verwaltungsgesellschaf fur Grundbesitz MBH shall not exercise their voting rights in the event of an early election or re-election of individual share- holder representatives or in the event of the election of substi- tute members, insofar as a resolution is adopted on the appointment or reappointment or replacement of a Supervi- sory Board member in whose original election they did not exercise their voting rights.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Pursuant to Section 31 Subsection 2 German Co-Determina- tion Act (MITBESTG), the Supervisory Board appoints the members of the Board of Management by a majority compris- ing at least two thirds of the votes of its members.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If an appointment is not made in this way, the Mediation Committee of the Supervisory Board will submit a proposal for the appointment to the Supervisory Board within one month of the vote in which the required majority was not achieved.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Supervisory Board then appoints the members of the Board of Management by a majority of the votes cast by its members.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If an appointment is still not forthcoming, the Chairman of the Supervisory Board then has two votes in a new vote.

This pro- cedure is to apply analogously to the revocation of the appoint- ment of members of the Board of Management.

Material agreements subject to a change of control The following signifcant agreements are in efect which con- tain provisions in the event of a change of control at Daimler Truck Holding AG, such as may occur, among other things, as the result of a takeover bid:  a non-utilized syndicated credit facility totaling 5.0 billion concluded in August 2021, and a non-utilized revolving facil- ity of 1.0 billion concluded in September 2022.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Subsidiary within the meaning of the named credit facilities is understood as, in each case in rela- tion to a company, another company (i) that is directly or indirectly controlled by the frst-mentioned company, (ii) in which more than 50.00% of the subscribed capital (or other equity) is held directly or indirectly by the frst-mentioned company, or (iii) which is a subsidiary of another subsidiary of the frst-mentioned company.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Control within the meaning of the syndicated credit facilities is understood as, in each case, (i) the right to determine the afairs of a company, (ii) the right to control the composition of the Board of Man- agement or similar boards, or (iii) the right to control the composition of the Supervisory Board (to the extent elected by the shareholders).

Classify the following text:
According to Recommendation C.4 GCGC 2019, a Supervi- sory Board member who is not a member of any manage- ment board of a listed company shall not accept more than fve supervisory board mandates at non-group listed compa- nies or comparable functions, with an appointment as chair of the supervisory board counting twice.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
According to Rec- ommendation C.5 GCGC 2019, members of the management boards of listed companies shall not accept in total more than two supervisory board mandates at non-group listed companies or comparable functions and shall not accept the chairmanship of a supervisory board at a non-group listed company.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Instead of observing the recommended total num- ber of mandates for members of the Board of Management and the Supervisory Board as a rigid upper limit, it should be possible to consider each individual case in order to assess whether the number of mandates held, which are relevant within the meaning of the Code, appears appropriate.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In this context, the individual workload to be expected as a result of the mandates accepted should be taken into account, which may vary depending on the mandate.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Risk and compliance management, internal control system and internal audit in the Group The Daimler Truck Group has internal control, risk and compli- ance management systems that are commensurate with the size and global presence of the Company and the scope of its business activities and that is geared towards the continuous and systematic management of entrepreneurial risks and opportunities.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The risk management system is one component of the Group- wide planning, controlling and reporting process.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This is to ensure that the Company management recognizes signifcant risks at an early stage and can initiate corrective actions in good time.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the context of the separation of the commercial vehicle business from the Mercedes-Benz Group in the 2021 fnancial year, Mercedes-Benz Group AG (formerly Daimler AG), Mercedes-Benz Verwaltungsgesellschaf fur Grundbesitz MBH (formerly Daimler Verwaltungsgesellschaf fur Grundbesitz MBH) and Daimler Truck Holding AG entered into a so-called deconsolidation agreement on August 6, 2021, as an annex to the spin-of and hive-down agreement, which took efect upon entry of the spin-of and hive-down in the commercial register of Mercedes-Benz Group AG as transferring legal entity on December 9, 2021.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This is intended to ensure that a de-facto majority of Mercedes-Benz Group AG at the General Meeting of Daimler Truck Holding AG does not lead to a control rela- tionship and a related full consolidation obligation of Daimler Truck Holding AG at Mercedes-Benz Group AG.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
To this end, the deconsolidation agreement provides, among other things, that Mercedes-Benz Group AG and Mercedes-Benz Verwaltungs- gesellschaf fur Grundbesitz MBH undertake not to exercise their voting rights in the election of two out of ten shareholder representatives to the Supervisory Board of Daimler Truck Holding AG at the General Meeting of Daimler Truck Holding AG.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, the agreement provides that Mercedes-Benz Group AG and Mercedes-Benz Verwaltungsgesellschaf fur Grundbesitz MBH shall not exercise their voting rights in the event of an early election or re-election of individual share- holder representatives or in the event of the election of substi- tute members, insofar as a resolution is adopted on the appointment or reappointment or replacement of a Supervi- sory Board member in whose original election they did not exercise their voting rights.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This also applies to resolutions on the dismissal of Supervisory Board members, insofar as they did not exercise their voting rights in the election of the rele- vant Supervisory Board members on the basis of the deconsol- idation agreement.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The composition of the Board of Management refects the par- ticipation requirement of Section 76 Subsection 3a German Stock Corporation Act (AKTG), introduced by the Act to Sup- plement and Amend the Regulations for the Equal Partici- pation of Women and Men in Management Positions in the Pri- vate and Public Sectors (Second Management Positions Act or FuPOG II), according to which at least one woman and one man must be a member of the Board of Management of com- panies subject to this provision.

Classify the following text:
The Board of Management of a listed or co-determined com- pany must in turn set targets for the proportion of women at the two management levels below the Board of Management.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the proportion of women is below 30% at the date when the Board of Management determines the targets, the targets may no longer fall below the proportion already achieved.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
At the same time as the targets are set, deadlines for their achieve- ment must be determined, which may not exceed fve years.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
By resolution of December10, 2021, the Board of Management of Daimler Truck Holding AG set a target proportion of women of 0% for the frst and second management levels below the Board of Management and a deadline of December 31, 2025.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Board of Management was of the opinion that it was not reasonable to set a target for the proportion of women because the structure of the particular company needs to be taken into account when determining this target fgure.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Daimler Truck Holding AG is structured as a management holding com- pany with the Board of Management and which provides man- agement services in the Group.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Below the level of the Board of Management, the Company  apart from a few employees with dual employment contracts  has no employees of its own.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the reporting period, Daimler Truck Holding AG had a total of less than fve employees below the Board of Management, all of whom had a dual employment contract with Daimler Truck AG.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
According to current planning, no personnel increase is envisaged for Daimler Truck Holding AG.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Second Manage- ment Positions Act (FuPOG II) assumes a larger number of employees and therefore also a larger number of management positions to be flled.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For this reason, setting the target fgure of 0% appears justifed exceptionally.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, setting the target of 0% does not constitute a violation of the deterioration requirement.

Generally, and subject to the disclosure of an exception in the Declaration of Compliance with the GCGC pursuant to Section161 of the German Stock Corporation Act (AKTG), members of the Board of Management of listed companies shall not hold more than two supervisory board mandates in listed companies or comparable functions, nor shall they hold the chair of the supervisory board in a non-group listed company.

Classify the following text:
According to the recommendations of the GCGC on the inde- pendence of the members of the Supervisory Board, on the shareholder side, the Supervisory Board is to include what it considers to be an appropriate number of independent members  also taking into account the shareholder struc- ture.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
A member is to be considered independent in this sense if they are independent of the Company and its Board of Management, and of any controlling shareholder.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
There is no controlling shareholder in this sense at the Company; against the background of the deconsolidation agreement concluded with Mercedes-Benz Group AG, in particular Mercedes-Benz Group AG is also not to be regarded as a controlling shareholder.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The GCGC recommends that more than half of the share- holder representatives are to be independent of the Com- pany and its Board of Management  and that these mem- bers must always include the Chairman of the Supervisory Board, the Chairman of the Audit Committee and the Chair- man of the committee that deals with the remuneration for the Board of Management.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Within the meaning of this rec- ommendation, a Supervisory Board member is to be consid- ered independent, if they have no personal or business rela- tionship with the Company or its Board of Management that may cause a substantial and not merely temporary confict of interest.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
It is up to the shareholder representatives side of the Super- visory Board to assess the independence of its members.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Four indicators of a possible lack of independence are to be considered (membership of the Board of Management within a period of two years prior to the appointment as member of the Supervisory Board; a material business relationship with the Company or an entity dependent on it, e.g., as a cus- tomer, supplier, creditor or advisor; a close family relation- ship with a member of the Board of Management; member- ship in the Supervisory Board for more than twelve years  all criteria apply both to Supervisory Board members themselves and to their relevant close family members).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
At the same time, the shareholder representative side is expressly granted the right to consider a Supervisory Board member independent if one or even multiple indicators apply, although this assessment is to be explained in the Declaration on Corporate Governance.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Supervisory Board has concluded that  with the excep- tion of Renata Jungo Brungger and Harald Wilhelm  all shareholder representatives in ofce as of December 31, 2022 are independent of Daimler Truck Holding AG and its Board of Management, including, in particular, the Chairman of the Supervisory Board, who is also Chairman of the Presi- dential and Remuneration Committee, and the Chairman of the Audit Committee.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Other than the two exceptions mentioned, also taking into account the indicators of the GCGC, none of the shareholder representatives has a personal or commercial relationship with Daimler Truck Holding AG or its Board of Management that could give rise to a material confict of interest that is not merely temporary in nature.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
With regard to Renata Jungo Brungger and Harald Wilhelm, it should be noted that both, as acting members of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz Group AG (i.e., in a responsible function of a company outside the Group), maintain a signifcant business relationship with the Company or a company dependent on this Company due to the extensive contractual interrelation- ships that exist between the two groups also since the spin- of became efective in December 2021.

Classify the following text:

Classify the following text:
Principles of consolidation The Consolidated Financial Statements include the fnancial statements of Daimler Truck Holding AG and the fnancial statements of all subsidiaries, including structured entities, which are directly or indirectly controlled by Daimler Truck Holding AG.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Control exists if the parent company has the power of decision over a subsidiary based on voting rights or other rights, if it participates in positive and negative variable returns from a subsidiary, and if it can afect these returns by its power of decision.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Structured entities which are controlled also have to be con- solidated.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Accordingly, the assets and liabilities are recognized in the Consolidated Statement of Financial Position.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Struc- tured entities are entities which have been designed so that voting or similar rights are not relevant in deciding who con- trols the entity.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This is the case for example if voting rights relate to administrative tasks only and the relevant activities are directed by means of contractual arrangements.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The fnancial statements of consolidated subsidiaries which are included in the Consolidated Financial Statements are pre- pared as of the reporting date of the Consolidated Financial Statements.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The fnancial statements of Daimler Truck Hold- ing AG and its subsidiaries included in the Consolidated Finan- cial Statements are prepared using uniform recognition and measurement principles.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
All intra-Group assets and liabilities, equity, income and expenses, except gains and losses from foreign currency translation, as well as cash fows from trans- actions between consolidated entities, are eliminated in the course of the consolidation process.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Investments in associated companies, joint ventures or joint operations An associated company is an entity over which the Group has signifcant infuence.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Signifcant infuence is the power to par- ticipate in the fnancial and operating policy decisions of the investee.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Associated companies are generally accounted for using the equity method.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For entities over which the Daimler Truck Group has joint con- trol together with a partner (joint arrangements), it is neces- sary to diferentiate whether a joint operation or a joint ven- ture exists.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In a joint venture, the parties that have joint control of the arrangement have rights to the net assets of the arrangement.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For joint ventures, the equity method has to be applied.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
A joint operation exists when the jointly controlling parties have direct rights to the assets and obligations for the liabilities.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In this case, the prorated assets and liabilities and the prorated income and expenses are generally to be recog- nized (proportionate consolidation).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Joint operations that have no signifcant impact on the Consolidated Financial State- ments are generally accounted for using the equity method.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Entities measured at amortized cost Subsidiaries, associated companies, joint ventures and joint operations whose business is non-active or of low volume and that individually and in sum are not material for the Group and the fair presentation of proftability, liquidity and capital resources and fnancial position are generally measured at amortized cost in the Consolidated Financial Statements.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Foreign currency translation Transactions in foreign currency are translated at the relevant foreign exchange rates prevailing at the transaction date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In subsequent periods, assets and liabilities denominated in for- eign currency are translated using period-end exchange rates; gains and losses from this measurement are recognized in proft or loss (except for gains and losses resulting from the translation of equity instruments measured at fair value through other comprehensive income, which are recognized in other comprehensive income).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Assets and liabilities of foreign companies for which the func- tional currency is not the Euro are translated into Euros using period-end exchange rates.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The translation adjustments are presented in other comprehensive income.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The components of equity are translated using historical rates.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Consolidated Statement of Income and the Consolidated Statement of Cash Flows are translated into Euros using the average exchange during the quarters of the fnancial years 2022 and 2021.

Classify the following text:

Classify the following text:
This generally occurs at the time when the customer takes possession of the products.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Generally, payment from sales of vehicles, service parts and other related products is made when the customer obtains control of these products.

Classify the following text:
Receivables from fnancial services.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Revenue recognition from the sale of vehicles for which the Group enters into a repurchase obligation is dependent on the form of the repurchase agreement:  Sales of vehicles by which the Daimler Truck Group is obliged to repurchase the vehicles in the future are accounted for as operating leases.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This also applies to a call option that only grants the Daimler Truck Group the right to repurchase;  Sales of vehicles including a put option (an entitys obliga- tion to repurchase the asset at the customers request) are reported as operating leases if the customer has a signif- cant economic incentive to exercise that right at contract inception.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Otherwise, a sale with a right of return is reported.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group considers several factors when assessing whether the customer has a signifcant economic incentive to exercise his right.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Amongst others, these are the relation between the agreed repurchase price and the expected future market value (at the time of repurchase) of the asset, or historical return rates.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Arrangements such as when the Daimler Truck Group provides customers with a guaranteed minimum resale value that they receive on resale (residual-value guarantee) do not constraint the customers in their ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the benefts from, the asset.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
At contract inception of a sale with a residual-value guarantee, revenue therefore has to be recognized, reduced by a potential com- pensation payment to the customer (revenue deferral).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Under a contract manufacturing agreement, the Daimler Truck Group sells assets to a third-party manufacturer from which the Daimler Truck Group buys back the manufactured products afer completion of the commissioned work.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the sale of the assets is not accompanied by the transfer of control to the third-party manufacturer, no revenue is recognized under IFRS15  Revenue from Contracts with Customers.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Group ofers extended, separately priced warranties for certain products as well as service and maintenance contracts.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Revenue from these contracts is deferred insofar as a cus- tomer has made an advance payment and is generally recog- nized over the contract period in proportion to the costs expected to be incurred based on historical information.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
A loss on these contracts is recognized in the current period if the expected costs for outstanding services under the contract exceed unearned revenue.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Usually, those contracts are paid in advance or in equal instalments over the contract term.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For multiple-element arrangements, such as when vehicles are sold with free or reduced-in-price maintenance programs or with free online services, the Group generally allocates reve- nue to the various elements based on their estimated relative stand-alone selling prices.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
To determine stand-alone selling prices, the Daimler Truck Group primarily uses price lists with consideration of average price reductions granted to its cus- tomers.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Vehicles may be initially sold to non-Group dealers.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Subse- quently, a customer may decide to enter into a leasing contract with Financial Services regarding such a vehicle.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The vehicle is therefore sold by the non-Group dealer to Financial Services and a leasing contract is entered into with the customer.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
When control of the vehicle is transferred to the non-Group dealer, the Daimler Truck Group recognizes revenue from the sale of the vehicle.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The incremental cost of obtaining contracts is recognized as an expense when incurred if the amortization period would be no longer than one year.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group does not adjust the promised amount of consideration for the efects of a signifcant fnancing com- ponent if at contract inception it is expected that the period between the transfer of a promised good or service to a cus- tomer and payment by the customer will be no longer than one year.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Revenue also includes revenue from the rental and leasing business as well as interest from the Financial Services busi- ness.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Revenue generated from operating leases is recognized on a straight-line basis over the periods of the contracts.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In addition, sales revenue is generated at the end of lease con- tracts from the subsequent sale of the vehicles.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group uses a variety of sales-promotion pro- grams dependent on various market conditions in individual countries as well as the respective product life cycles and product-related factors (such as amounts of discounts ofered by competitors, excess industry production capacity, the inten- sity of market competition and consumer demand for the prod- ucts).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
These programs comprise cash ofers to dealers and customers, as well as lease subsidies or loans at reduced inter- est rates, which are reported as follows:  Revenue is recognized net of sales reductions such as cash discounts and sales incentives granted.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
When loans are issued below market rates, the related receivables are recognized at present value (using market rates) and revenue is reduced for the interest incentive granted.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If subsidized leasing fees are agreed upon in connection with fnance leases, revenue from the sale of a vehicle is reduced by the amount of the interest incentive granted.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Research and non-capitalized development costs Expenditure for research and development that does not meet the conditions for capitalization according to IAS 38 Intangible Assets is expensed as incurred.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Borrowing costs Borrowing costs are expensed as incurred unless they are directly attributable to the acquisition, construction or produc- tion of a qualifying asset and are therefore part of the cost of that asset.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Depreciation of the capitalized borrowing costs is presented within cost of sales.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Government grants Government grants related to assets are deducted from the carrying amount of the asset and are recognized in earnings over the life of a depreciable asset as a reduced depreciation expense.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Government grants which compensate the Group for expenses are recognized as other operating income in the same period as the expenses themselves.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Profit/loss on equity-method investments, net This item includes all income and expenses in connection with investments accounted for using the equity method.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In addi- tion to the prorated profts and losses from fnancial invest- ments, it also includes profts and losses resulting from the sale of equity interests or the remeasurement of equity inter- ests following a loss of signifcant infuence or joint control.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Groups share of dilution gains and losses resulting from the Groups non-participation or disproportion- ately low participation in capital measures of companies in which shares are held and are accounted for using the equity method are also included in proft/loss on equity-method investments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This item also includes impairment losses and/or gains on the reversal of such impairments of equity-method investments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Other financial income/expense, net Other fnancial income/expense, net includes all income and expense from fnancial transactions which are included neither in interest income nor in interest expense, and which for the Financial Services segment are included neither in revenue nor in cost of sales.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For example, the expense from the compound- ing of interest on provisions for other risks is recognized in this line item.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, income and expenses from equity interests are included in other fnancial income/expense, net, if such income or expenses are not presented under equity-method investments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Interest income and interest expense Interest income and interest expense include interest income from investments in securities and from cash and cash equiva- lents as well as interest expense from liabilities.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, interest and changes in fair values related to interest rate hedging activities as well as income and expense resulting from the allocation of premiums and discounts are included.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The interest components of defned beneft pension obligations and other similar obligations as well as of the plan assets avail- able to cover these obligations and interest on supplementary income tax payments or reimbursements are also presented in this line item.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For the Financial Services segment, interest income and expense and gains or losses from derivative fnancial instru- ments related to the fnancial services business are disclosed under revenue and cost of sales respectively.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Current income taxes are calculated based on the respective local taxable income and local tax laws for the period.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In addi- tion, current income taxes include amounts for uncertain tax payments or tax refunds for periods not yet fnally assessed, but exclude interest expenses, interest refunds and penalties concerning uncertain income tax positions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If it is probable that a taxation authority will not accept an uncertain tax treatment, a tax expense or income for uncertain income tax positions is recognized.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The amount is based on the best estimate of the expected tax payment (expected value or most likely amount).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Only in the case of tax loss carryfor- wards or unused tax credits, no current tax liabilities or tax refunds are recognized for these uncertain tax positions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Instead, the deferred tax assets for the unused tax loss carry- forwards or tax credits are adjusted.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Current tax liabilities and assets are presented as income tax liabilities and assets on the balance sheet.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Deferred tax assets or liabilities are calculated on the basis of temporary diferences between the tax basis and the carrying amount of assets and liabilities as well as on unused tax loss carryforwards and unused tax credits.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
No deferred tax assets or liabilities are recognized for temporary diferences resulting from the initial recognition of assets or liabilities in a trans- action which neither constitutes a business combination nor afects accounting or taxable proft (initial recognition exemp- tion).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group does not recognize deferred tax assets on temporary diferences, tax loss carryforwards and tax credits if it is not probable that future taxable income will be available against which the deductible temporary difer- ences, tax loss carryforwards and tax credits can be utilized.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Measurement is based on the tax rates that are expected to apply to the period when the asset is realized or the liability is settled.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For this purpose, tax rates and tax laws are used which have been substantively enacted at the reporting date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Changes in deferred tax assets and liabilities are generally rec- ognized in deferred tax income or expense inside proft or loss, except for changes recognized in other comprehensive income or directly in equity.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Deferred tax assets and liabilities are presented separately as non-current items on the balance sheet.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Deferred tax liabilities for taxable temporary diferences asso- ciated with investments in subsidiaries, branches, associates and interests in joint arrangements are not recognized if the Daimler Truck Group is able to control the timing of the rever- sal of the temporary diference and it is probable that the tem- porary diference will not reverse in the foreseeable future.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Earnings per share Basic earnings per share are calculated by dividing proft attributable to shareholders of the Daimler Truck Group by the weighted-average number of shares outstanding.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
As there were no dilutive efects in 2022 and 2021, diluted earnings per share correspond to basic earnings per share in these years.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Intangible assets Intangible assets are measured at acquisition or manufacturing cost less accumulated amortization.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If necessary, accumulated impairment losses are recognized.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Intangible assets with indefnite useful lives are reviewed annu- ally to determine whether indefnite-life assessment continues to be appropriate.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If not, the change in the useful-life assess- ment from indefnite to fnite is made on a prospective basis.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Development costs for vehicles and components are recog- nized if the conditions for capitalization according to IAS 38 are met.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Subsequent to initial recognition, the asset is carried at cost less accumulated amortization and accumulated impairment losses.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Capitalized development costs include all direct costs and allocable overheads and are amortized on a straight-line basis over the expected product life cycle (in gen- eral with a maximum amortization period of ten years).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Amorti- zation of capitalized development costs is an element of manu- facturing costs and is allocated to those vehicles and components by which they were generated and is included in cost of sales when the inventory (vehicles) is sold.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Other intangible assets with fnite useful lives are generally amortized on a straight-line basis over their useful lives (three to ten years).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The amortization period for intangible assets with fnite useful lives is reviewed at least at each year-end.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Changes in expected useful lives are treated as changes in accounting estimates.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The amortization expense on intangible assets with fnite useful lives is recognized in functional costs.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
With acquisitions of businesses, goodwill represents the excess of the consideration transferred over the fair values assigned to the identifable assets proportionally acquired and liabilities assumed.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the amount is less than the fair value of the individually identifable acquired assets and liabilities, the diference is recognized directly through proft or loss as an acquisition at a price below market value.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Goodwill is accounted for at the subsidiaries in the functional currency of each subsidiary.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In connection with obtaining control, non-controlling interest in the acquiree is principally recognized at the proportionate share of the acquirees identifable assets, which are measured at fair value.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Property, plant and equipment Property, plant and equipment are measured at acquisition or manufacturing costs less accumulated depreciation.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If neces- sary, accumulated impairment losses are recognized.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The costs of internally produced equipment and facilities include all direct costs and allocable overheads.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Acquisition or manufacturing costs include estimated costs, if any, of disman- tling, restoration and removals.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
D.07 Useful lives of property, plant and equipment Leasing Leases include all contracts that transfer the right-to-use a specifed asset for a stated period of time in exchange for con- sideration, even if the right-to-use such asset is not explicitly described in the contract.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Group is a lessee mainly of real-estate properties and a lessor of its products.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Daimler Truck Group as lessee The Daimler Truck Group as a lessee recognizes, for generally all lease contracts, right-of-use assets as well as correspond- ing leasing liabilities for the outstanding lease payments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
According to IFRS16  Leases, a lessee may elect, for leases with a lease term of twelve months or less (short-term leases) and for leases for which the underlying asset is of low value, not to recognize a right-of-use asset and a lease liability.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group applies both recognition exemptions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The lease payments associated with those leases are generally rec- ognized as an expense on a straight-line basis over the lease term or on another systematic basis if appropriate.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Right-of-use assets, which are included under property, plant and equipment, are initially measured at cost.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The cost of a right-of-use asset comprises the amount of the initial measure- ment of the lease liability, any lease payments made at or before the commencement date, less any lease incentives received from the lessor, any initial direct costs and an esti- mate of costs to be incurred in dismantling or removing the underlying asset.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Lease liabilities, which are assigned to fnancing liabilities, are measured initially at the present value of the lease payments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The lease liabilities include the following lease payments:  variable lease payments linked to an index or interest rate;  amounts expected to be payable under residual-value guar- antees;  the exercise price of purchase options, when exercise is esti- mated to be reasonably certain and  contractual penalties for the termination of a lease if the lease term refects the exercise of a termination option.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group generally also applies the option for contracts comprising lease components as well as non-lease components not to split these components.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Lease payments are discounted at the rate implicit in the lease if that rate can readily be determined.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Otherwise, discounting is at the incremental borrowing rate.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The incremental borrow- ing rate, which is mainly applied at the Daimler Truck Group, is based on risk-adjusted interest rates and determined for the respective lease terms and currencies.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
As the cash fow pat- tern of the reference interest rates (bullet bonds) does not cor- respond to the cash fow pattern of a lease contract (annuity), we use a duration adjustment in order to account for that dif- ference.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
A right-of-use asset is subsequently measured at cost less any accumulated depreciation and, if necessary, any accumulated impairment.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the lease transfers ownership of the underlying asset to the lessee at the end of the lease term or if the cost of the right-of-use asset refects that the lessee will exercise a purchase option, the right-of-use asset is depreciated to the end of the useful life of the underlying asset.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Otherwise, the right-of-use asset is depreciated to the end of the lease term.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the subsequent measurement of a lease liability, the carry- ing amount is increased to refect interest on the lease liability (through proft or loss) and reduced by lease payments made.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
According to IFRS16, the depreciation of right-of-use assets is recognized within functional costs.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The interest due on the lease liability is a component of interest expense.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Extension and termination options are part of a number of leases, particularly of real-estate.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Such contract terms ofer the Daimler Truck Group the greatest possible fexibility.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In deter- mining the lease term, those options are only considered if they are reasonably certain.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Sale and leaseback In a sale and leaseback transaction, the requirements of IFRS15 are applied to ascertain whether the transfer of an asset has to be accounted for as a sale.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the transfer of an asset does not satisfy the requirements of IFRS15 to be accounted for as a sale of the asset, the trans- ferred asset is still recognized and a fnancial liability is recog- nized equal to the transfer proceeds in accordance with IFRS 9  Financial instruments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Accordingly, only the amount of any gain or loss that relates to the rights transferred to the buyer-lessor is recog- nized.

Classify the following text:
Daimler Truck Group as lessor Based on the risk and rewards associated with a leased asset, it is assessed whether economic ownership of the leased asset is transferred to the lessee (so-called fnance leases) or remains with the lessor (so-called operating leases).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
by which economic ownership of the vehicle remains at the Daimler Truck Group, relate to vehicles that the Group produces itself and leases to third-parties.

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Otherwise, a sale with a right of return is reported.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Amongst others, these are the relation between repurchase price and the expected future market value (at the time of repurchase) of the asset or historical return rates.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
As part of the residual-value management process, especially for operating lease contracts, certain assumptions are regu- larly made at local and corporate levels regarding the expected level of prices, based upon which the vehicles to be returned in the leasing business are evaluated.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If changing market devel- opments lead to a negative deviation from assumptions, there is a risk of lower residual-values of used vehicles.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Depending on the region and the current market situation, the measures taken generally include continuous market monitoring as well as, if required, price-setting strategies or sales-promotion measures designed to regulate vehicle inventories.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The quality of market forecasts is verifed by regular comparisons of inter- nal and external sources, and, if required, the determination of residual-values is adjusted and further developed with regard to methods, processes and systems.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the case of accounting as an operating lease, these vehicles are capitalized at (depreciated) cost of production under leased equipment and are depreciated over the contract term on a straight-line basis with consideration of the expected residual-values.

Classify the following text:
Equity-method investments On the date of acquisition, a positive diference between cost of acquisition and the Daimler Truck Groups share of the fair values of the identifable assets and liabilities of the associated company or joint venture is determined and recognized as investor level goodwill.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The goodwill is included in the carrying amount of the equity-method investment.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If an equity interest in an existing associated company is increased without change in signifcant infuence, goodwill is determined only for the additionally acquired interest; the previous investment is not remeasured at fair value.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group reviews on each reporting date whether there is any objective indication of impairments or impairment reversals of equity-method investments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If such indications exist, the Group determines the impairment loss or reversal to be recognized.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the carrying amount exceeds the recoverable amount of an investment, the carrying amount is written down to the recoverable amount.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The recoverable amount is the greater of fair value less costs to sell and value in use.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
An impairment reversal is carried out if there is objec- tive evidence for an impairment reversal.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If such an assess- ment is made, the recoverable amount is remeasured.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Impairment test of non-current non-financial assets At December 31, 2022, the Daimler Truck Group assessed whether there was an indication that an asset might be impaired or whether there was an indication that a previously recognized impairment loss might be reversed.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If such indica- tion exists, the Daimler Truck Group estimates the recoverable amount of the asset.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The recoverable amount is determined for each individual asset unless the asset generates cash infows that are not largely independent of those from other assets or groups of assets (cash-generating units or CGU(s)).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Goodwill and other intangible assets with indefnite useful lives are tested at least annually for impairment; this takes place at the level of the cash-generating units.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the carrying amount of an asset or of a cash-generating unit exceeds the recoverable amount, an impairment loss is recognized for the diference.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The recoverable amount is the higher of fair value less costs to sell and value in use.

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Accident-free driving means compre- hensive protection for all road users.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In order to make this vision reality, alongside its existing top safety features within its vehicles, the Daimler Truck Group is focusing on the further development of its driver assistance and autonomous driving systems in particular.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Our activities to drive transformation through autonomous trucking solutions are especially refected within the TORC Robotics, Inc. CGU.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Value in use is measured by discounting expected future cash fows from the continuing use of the cash-generating units using a risk-adjusted interest rate.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Future cash fows are deter- mined on the basis of the long-term planning, which are approved by management and are valid at the date when the impairment test is conducted.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In general, the planning period covers fve-year period.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Terminal value derivations are based on a steady state, which refects start-up businesses such as the TORC Robotics, Inc. CGU.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Planning is based on expecta- tions regarding future market shares, the general development of respective markets as well as the products proftability.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The derivation of value in use includes risk assessments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The assumptions for the plans are validated by considering histori- cal trends and external data sources (including, but not limited to, market surveys).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The efects of IFRS16 is refected consis- tently within the calculation of the value in use, as well as in measuring the net assets.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For the automotive business CGUs, the rounded risk-ad- justed interest rates used to discount cash fows are calcu- lated for each cash-generating unit at 9.0% afer taxes/12.0% before taxes (December 31, 2021: 8.0% afer taxes/12.0% before taxes).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The main assumptions used for discounted cash fows are sales trends and return on sales, which, for most CGUs, on average increase over the planning period.

Classify the following text:
Return on sales are considered on an adjusted basis by excluding any special or one-of efects.

Classify the following text:
The main assumptions used are the return on equity and growth in new customers, which on average increase over the plan- ning period.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Return on equity is considered on an adjusted basis by excluding any special or one-of efects.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For the TORC Robotics, Inc. CGU, a separate risk-adjusted rate of18.5% afer taxes/25.0% before taxes is applied, rep- resenting the CGU as a start-up enterprise (December 31, 2021:17.5% afer taxes/23.5% before taxes).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The discount rate for the Financial Services CGU represents the cost of equity, whereas the risk-adjusted interest rate for the cash-generating units of the automotive business and TORC Robotics, Inc. are based on the weighted-average cost of capital (WACC).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This is calculated based on the capital asset pricing model (CAPM), taking into account current market expectations.

Periods not covered by the forecast are taken into account by recognizing a residual value (terminal value), which does not include any growth rates.

In addition, several sensitivity analy- ses are conducted.

Classify the following text:

Truck and bus indus- tries are subject to cyclical fuctuations, and as a result, react to varying degrees, depending on region and sector.
