Classify the following text:
Non-current assets held for sale and disposal groups The Group classifes non-current assets or disposal groups as held for sale if the carrying amount will be recovered princi- pally through a sale transaction rather than through continuing use.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In this case, the assets or disposal groups are no longer depreciated but measured at the lower of carrying amount and fair value less costs to sell.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Immediately before classifcation as held for sale, the carrying amount of the asset is deter- mined in accordance with the applicable individual require- ments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If fair value less costs to sell subsequently increases, any impairment loss previously recognized is reversed.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This reversal is restricted to the impairment loss previously recog- nized for the asset or disposal group concerned.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Group generally discloses these assets or disposal groups separately in the Consolidated Statement of Financial Position.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Inventories Inventories are measured at the lower of acquisition or manu- facturing cost and net realizable value.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The net realizable value is the expected sales price less estimated costs of completion and estimated costs to sell.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The acquisition or manufacturing costs of inventories are generally based on the specifc identi- fcation method and include costs incurred in acquiring the inventories and bringing them to their present location and condition.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Acquisition or manufacturing costs for large num- bers of inventories that are interchangeable are allocated under the average-cost formula.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the case of manufactured inventories and work in progress, manufacturing cost also includes production overheads based on normal capacity.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financial instruments A fnancial instrument is any contract that gives rise to a fnan- cial asset of one entity and a fnancial liability or equity instru- ment of another entity.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financial instruments in the form of fnancial assets and fnancial liabilities are generally presented separately.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financial instruments are recognized as soon as the Daimler Truck Group becomes a party to the contractual provisions of the fnancial instrument.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the case of purchases or sales of fnancial assets through the regular market, the Daimler Truck Group uses the transaction date as the date of initial recognition or derecognition.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Upon initial recognition, fnancial instruments are measured at fair value.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For the purpose of subsequent measurement, fnan- cial instruments are allocated to one of the categories men- tioned in IFRS 9 (fnancial assets measured at amortized cost, fnancial assets measured at fair value through other compre- hensive income and fnancial assets measured at fair value through proft or loss).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Transaction costs directly attributable to acquisition or issuance are considered when determining the carrying amount if the fnancial instruments are not mea- sured at fair value through proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financial assets Financial assets primarily comprise receivables from fnancial services, trade receivables, receivables from banks, cash on hand, derivative fnancial assets, marketable securities and similar investments and fnancial investments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The classifca- tion of fnancial instruments is based on the business model in which these instruments are held and on their contractual cash fows.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The determination of the business model is carried out at the portfolio level and is based on managements intention and past transaction patterns.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Assessments of the contractual cash fows are made on an instrument-by-instrument basis.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financial assets at fair value through proft or loss: Financial assets at fair value through proft or loss include fnancial assets with cash fows other than those of principal and interest on the nominal amount outstanding.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, fnancial assets that are held in a business model other than hold to collect or hold to collect and sell are included here.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In addition, derivatives, including embedded derivatives sepa- rated from the host contract, which are not classifed as hedging instruments in hedge accounting, as well as fnancial assets acquired for the purpose of selling in the short term that are classifed as held for trading, are included here.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Gains or losses on these fnancial assets are recognized in proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financial assets at amortized cost: Financial assets at amortized cost are non-derivative fnancial assets with contractual cash fows that consist solely of pay- ments of principal and interest on the nominal amount out- standing and which are held with the aim of collecting the con- tractual cash fows, such as receivables from fnancial services, trade receivables or cash and cash equivalents (business model hold to collect).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Cash and cash equivalents consist primarily of cash on hand, checks and demand deposits at banks, as well as debt instruments and certifcates of deposits with a remaining term when acquired of up to three months, which are not subject to any material value fuctuations.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Cash and cash equivalents correspond with the classifcation in the Consolidated Statement of Cash Flows.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Afer initial recognition, fnancial assets at amortized cost are subsequently carried at amortized cost using the efective interest method less any loss allowances.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Gains and losses are recognized in the Consolidated Statement of Income when the fnancial assets at amortized cost are impaired or derecog- nized.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Interest efects on the application of the efective inter- est method are also recognized in proft or loss, as well as the efects of currency translation.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income are non-derivative fnancial assets with contractual cash fows that consist solely of payments of principal and interest on the nominal amount outstanding and which are held to collect the contractual cash fows as well as to sell the fnancial assets, e.g.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
to achieve a defned liquidity target (busi- ness model hold to collect and sell).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This category also includes equity instruments not held for trading for which the option to present changes in the fair value of the instrument within other comprehensive income has been applied.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Afer initial measurement, fnancial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income are measured at fair value, with unrealized gains or losses being recognized in other compre- hensive income.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Upon disposal of debt instruments, the accu- mulated gains and losses recognized in other comprehensive income resulting from measurement at fair value are recog- nized in proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Interest earned on fnancial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income is generally reported as interest income using the efective interest method.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Changes in the fair value of equity instruments mea- sured at fair value through other comprehensive income are not recycled to proft or loss, but reclassifed to retained earn- ings upon disposal.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Dividends are recognized in proft or loss when the right to payment has been established.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Impairment of financial assets At each reporting date, a loss allowance is recognized for fnancial assets, loan commitments and fnancial guarantees, other than those to be measured at fair value through proft or loss, refecting expected losses for these instruments.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Expected credit losses are allocated using three stages: Stage1 includes all contracts with no signifcant increase in credit risk since initial recognition and usually includes new acquisitions and contracts with fewer than 31 days past due date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The portion of the lifetime expected credit losses result- ing from default events possible within the next12 months is recognized.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Stage 2: Expected credit losses over the lifetime  not credit impaired If a fnancial asset has a signifcant increase in credit risk since initial recognition but is not yet credit impaired, it is moved to stage 2 and measured at lifetime expected credit loss, which is defned as the expected credit loss that results from all possi- ble default events over the expected life of a fnancial instru- ment.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Stage 3: Expected credit losses over the lifetime  credit impaired If a fnancial asset is defned as credit impaired or in default, it is transferred to stage 3 and measured at lifetime expected credit loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Objective evidence for a credit-impaired fnancial asset includes 91 days past due date and other information about signifcant fnancial difculties of the borrower.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The determination of whether a fnancial asset has experienced a signifcant increase in credit risk is based on an assessment of the probability of default, which is made at least quarterly, incorporating external credit rating information as well as internal information on the credit quality of the fnancial asset.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For debt instruments that are not receivables from fnancial services, a signifcant increase in credit risk is assessed, mainly based on past-due information or the probability of default.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
A fnancial asset is migrated to stage 2 if the assets credit risk has increased signifcantly compared to its credit risk at initial recognition.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The credit risk is assessed based on the probabil- ity of default.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For trade receivables, the simplifed approach is applied whereby all trade receivables are allocated to stage 2 initially.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Hence, no determination of signifcant increases in credit risk is necessary.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group applies the low credit risk exception to the stage allocation to quoted debt instruments with invest- ment-grade ratings.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
These debt instruments are always allo- cated to stage1.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In stages1 and 2, the efective interest revenue is calculated based on gross carrying amounts.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If a fnancial asset becomes credit impaired in stage 3, the efective interest revenue is cal- culated based on its net carrying amount (gross carrying amount adjusted for any loss allowance).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Measurement of expected credit losses.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Expected credit losses are measured as the probability- weighted present value of all cash shortfalls over the expected life of each fnancial asset.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

The estimation of these risk parameters incorporates all avail- able relevant information, not only historical and current loss data, but also reasonable and supportable forward-looking information refected by the future expectation factors.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Derivative financial instruments and hedge accounting The Group uses derivative fnancial instruments exclusively for hedging fnancial risks that arise from its operating or fnanc- ing activities or liquidity management.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
These are mainly cur- rency risks and interest rate risks.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Embedded derivatives are principally separated from the host contract and recognized separately.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
However, embedded deriv- atives are not separated from the host contract if that host contract is a fnancial asset, if the Daimler Truck Group chooses to measure a hybrid contract at fair value through proft or loss, or if the embedded derivative is closely related to the host contract.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Derivative fnancial instruments are measured at fair value upon initial recognition and at each subsequent reporting date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The fair value of listed derivatives is equal to their positive or negative market value.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If a market value is not available, fair value is calculated using standard fnancial valuation models such as discounted cash fow or option-pricing models.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Deriva- tives are presented as assets if their fair value is positive and as liabilities if their fair value is negative.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the requirements for hedge accounting set out in IFRS 9 are met, the Daimler Truck Group designates and documents the hedge relationship from the date a derivative contract is entered into as a fair value hedge or a cash fow hedge.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In a fair value hedge, the changes in the fair value of a recognized asset or liability or an unrecognized frm commitment are hedged.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In a cash fow hedge, the variability of cash fows to be received or paid from expected transactions related to a recognized asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction is hedged.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The documentation of the hedging relationship includes the objectives and strategy of risk management, the type of hedging relationship, the nature of the risk being hedged, the identifcation of the eligible hedging instrument and the eligible hedged item, as well as an assessment of the efectiveness requirements comprising the risk-mitigating eco- nomic relationship, the absence of deteriorating efects from credit risk and the appropriate hedge ratio.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Hedging transac- tions are regularly assessed to determine whether the efec- tiveness requirements are met while they are designated.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Changes in the fair value of non-designated derivatives are rec- ognized in proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For fair value hedges, changes in the fair value of the hedged item and the derivative are recognized in proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For cash fow hedges, fair value changes in the efective portion of the hedging instrument are recognized afer tax in other comprehensive income.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Under IFRS 9, for cash fow hedges in procurement transac- tions expected with a high degree of probability, designation can be made for separable risk components of these non-f- nancial hedged items.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Under IFRS 9, with cash fow hedges, amounts recognized in other comprehensive income as efective hedging gains or losses from hedging instruments are removed from the reserves for derivative fnancial instruments and directly included in the initial cost or carrying amount of the hedged item at initial recognition if the hedged item, e.g.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
forecast transaction, results in the recognition of a non-fnancial asset or non-fnancial liability.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For other cash fow hedges, the accumulated hedging gains or losses from hedging instruments are reclassifed from the reserves for derivative fnancial instruments to the Consoli- dated Statement of Income when the hedged item afects proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The inefective portions of fair value changes are recognized directly in proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
This may apply for example to the time value of options, the forward element of a forward contract or cross-currency basis spreads.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Hedge relationships are to be discontinued prospectively if a particular hedge relationship ceases to meet the qualifying cri- teria for hedge accounting under IFRS 9.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Instances that require discontinuation of hedge accounting are, among others, changes to the designated hedged item, loss of the economic relationship between the hedged item and the hedging instru- ment, disposal or termination of the hedging instrument, or a revision of the documented risk management objective of a particular hedge relationship.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Accumulated hedging gains and losses from cash fow hedges are retained and are reclassifed from equity as described at maturity if the hedged future cash fows are still expected to occur.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Otherwise, accumulated hedging gains and losses are immediately reclassifed to proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If derivative fnancial instruments do not or no longer qualify for hedge accounting because the qualifying criteria for hedge accounting are not or are no longer met, the derivative fnan- cial instruments are classifed as held for trading and are mea- sured at fair value through proft or loss.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Pensions and similar obligations The measurement of defned beneft plans for pensions and other post-employment beneft obligations (medical care) in accordance with IAS19 Employee Benefts is based on the pro- jected unit credit method.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Plan assets invested to cover defned beneft pension obligations and other post-employ- ment beneft obligations (medical care) are measured at fair value and ofset against the corresponding obligations.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the beneft entitlements are covered by plan assets, a net amount is recognized from the fair value of the plan assets and the DBO is adjusted in relation to any efects of the asset ceiling.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The balance of defned beneft plans for pensions and other post-employment beneft obligations and plan assets (net pen- sion obligation or net pension assets) accrues interest at the discount rate used as a basis for the measurement of the gross pension obligation.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The resulting net interest expense or income is recognized in proft or loss under interest expense or interest income in the Consolidated Statement of Income.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The other expenses resulting from pension obligations and other post-employment beneft obligations (medical care), which mainly result from entitlements acquired during the year under review, are taken into consideration in the functional costs in the Consolidated Statement of Income.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Diferences between the assumptions made and actual developments as well as changes in actuarial assumptions for the measurement of defned beneft plans and similar obligations result in actuarial gains and losses, which have a direct impact on the Consoli- dated Statement of Financial Position and are recognized in Other Comprehensive Income.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The discount factors used to calculate the present values of defned beneft pension obligations are to be determined  with maturities and currencies matching the pension payments  by reference to market yields at the end of the reporting period on high-quality corporate bonds in the respective mar- kets.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For very long maturities, there are no high-quality corpo- rate bonds available as a benchmark.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The respective discount factors are estimated by extrapolating current market rates along the yield curve.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Gains or losses on the curtailment or settlement of a defned beneft plan are recognized in proft or loss when the curtail- ment or settlement occurs.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Provisions for other risks A provision is recognized when a liability to third-parties has been incurred, an outfow of resources is probable and the amount of the obligation can be reasonably estimated.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The amount recognized as a provision represents the best estimate of the obligation at the reporting date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Provisions with an origi- nal maturity of more than one year are discounted to the pres- ent value of the expenditures expected to settle the obligation at the end of the reporting period.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If the criteria of the regula- tions on recognition and measurement of provisions are not fulflled and the possibility of a cash outfow upon settlement is not unlikely, the item is to be presented as a contingent lia- bility, insofar as it is adequately measurable.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The amount dis- closed as a contingent liability represents the best estimate of the possible obligation at the reporting date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Provisions and contingent liabilities are regularly reviewed and adjusted as further information becomes available or circumstances change.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Estimates for accrued warranty costs are primarily based on historical experience.

Classify the following text:
Contract and refund liabilities Contract liabilities: A contract liability is an entitys obligation to transfer goods or services to a customer for which the entity has received con- sideration (or the amount is due) from the customer.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Contract liabilities occur at the Daimler Truck Group especially from prepaid service and maintenance contracts and extended warranties.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Refund liabilities: A refund liability occurs if the Daimler Truck Group receives consideration from a customer and expects to refund some or all of that consideration to the customer.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
A refund liability is measured at the amount of consideration received for which the Daimler Truck Group does not expect to be entitled and is thus not included in the transaction price.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Refund liabilities occur at the Daimler Truck Group especially in the following circumstances: Share-based payment Share-based payment comprises cash-settled liability awards.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Liability awards are measured at fair value at each balance sheet date until settlement and are classifed as provisions under consideration of vesting conditions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The proft or loss of the period equals the addition to and/or the reversal of the provision during the reporting period and the dividend equiva- lent paid during the period and is included in functional costs.

Presentation in the Consolidated Statement of Cash Flows The Daimler Truck Group uses the indirect method for the determination of cash fows from operating activities.

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The residual-values thus determined serve as a basis for depreciation; changes in residual-values lead either to prospective adjustments of the depreciation or, in the case of a signifcant decline in expected residual-values, to an impairment.

Classify the following text:
Performance Phantom Share Plan In 2022, the Daimler Truck Group adopted a Performance Phantom Share Plan (PPSP).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Under the PPSP, the Group grants phantom shares to eligible employees who are entitled to receive a cash payment afer four years.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
During the four- year term between the allocation of the preliminary phantom shares and the payout of the plan at the end of the term, the phantom shares earn a dividend equivalent in the amount of the dividends paid in the respective year to actual Daimler Truck Holding AG shares.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Based on the resolution passed at the Annual General Meeting in 2022 to not pay a dividend, a dividend equivalent was also not paid.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The payout amount at the end of the term is based on the (fnal) number of vested phantom shares (determined afer three years depending on the degree of achievement of certain key fgures) multiplied by the price of the Daimler Truck Hold- ing AG share (calculated as an average price over a specifed period at the end of the four-year plan term).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The vesting period is there-fore four years.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For the existing plans, the price of the Daimler Truck Holding AG share is limited to 2.5 times the price at the date of grant.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, the payout for the members of the Board of Management is additionally limited to 2.5 times the allotment value used to determine the prelimi- nary number of phantom shares.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The limitation of the payout for the members of the Board of Management also includes the dividend equivalents.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For the PPSP currently granted, target achievement is deter- mined on the basis of the relative share performance, which measures the price performance of an equity index (total shareholder return) based on a peer group including Daimler Truck Holding AG, and the return on sales (ROS) of the Daimler Truck Group compared with the ROS of a peer group (reve- nue-weighted average).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
For the current PPSP 2019 to 2021 inclusive, the share per- formance and the ROS for the periods up to the spin-of were calculated based on the performance of Mercedes-Benz Group AG (former Daimler AG), and for the periods afer the spin-of based on the performance of Daimler Truck Hold- ing AG.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the period from the frst trading day of the Daimler Truck Holding AG share on December10, 2021 to December 31, 2021, the average of the prices of the shares of Mercedes-Benz Group AG (former Daimler AG) and Daimler Truck Holding AG was taken into account for the relative share performance.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The performance of ROS was calculated until the end of 2021 based on Mercedes-Benz Group AG (former Daimler AG).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Special regulations apply to the PPSP 2019 to 2021 for the members of the Board of Management of Daimler Truck Hold- ing AG, who were previously members of the Board of Manage- ment of Mercedes-Benz Group AG (former Daimler AG): The Daimler Truck Group ROS must be higher than the same aver- age ROS of the peer group in order to obtain the same target achievement as the other plan participants.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In this case, the deviation of the average ROS of the Daimler Truck Group from the average ROS of the peer group multiplied by1.05 deter- mines the level of the success factor.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, a limit on target achievement was agreed upon the ROS reference parameter.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In the case of target achievement between195% and 200%, an additional comparison is made based on the achieved ROS in absolute terms.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Accordingly, if, in the third year of the performance period, the actual ROS of the Daimler Truck Commercial Vehicles business is below the strategic tar- get return value of 9%, the target achievement is restricted to 195%.

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The fair value of a fnancial instrument is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at the mea- surement date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Given the varying infuencing factors, the reported fair values can only be viewed as indicators of the prices that may actually be achieved on the market.

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The following methods and premises were used: Receivables from financial services The fair values of receivables from fnancial services with vari- able interest rates are estimated to be equal to the respective carrying amounts, because the agreed-upon interest rates and those available in the market do not signifcantly difer.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Trade receivables and cash and cash equivalents Due to the short terms of these fnancial instruments and the fundamentally lower credit risk, it is assumed that their fair values are equal to the carrying amounts.

General information on financial risks As a result of its businesses and the global nature of its opera- tions, the Daimler Truck Group is exposed to market risks from changes in foreign currency exchange rates and interest rates, while commodity price risks arise from procurement.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
A share price risk results from investments in listed companies.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In addition, the Daimler Truck Group is exposed to credit risks from its leasing and fnancing activities and from its other busi- ness operations (trade receivables).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Furthermore, the Daimler Truck Group is exposed to liquidity and country risks relating to its credit and market risks or a deterioration of its business operations or fnancial market disturbances.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
If these fnancial risks materialize, they could adversely afect the Daimler Truck Groups proftability, liquidity and capital resources, and fnan- cial position.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group has established internal policies for risk controlling procedures and for the use of fnancial instru- ments, including a clear segregation of duties with regard to fnancial activities, settlement, accounting and the related con- trolling.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The guidelines upon which the Daimler Truck Groups risk management processes for fnancial risks are based are designed to identify and analyze these risks throughout the Daimler Truck Group, to set appropriate risk limits and con- trols, and to monitor the risks by means of reliable and up-to- date administrative and information systems.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The guidelines and systems are regularly reviewed and adjusted to changes in markets and products.
Answer: Non-Environmental

The Daimler Truck Group manages and monitors these risks primarily through its operating and fnancing activities and, if required, through the use of derivative fnancial instruments.

The Daimler Truck Group uses derivative fnancial instruments exclusively for hedging fnancial risks that arise from its busi- ness operations or refnancing activities or liquidity manage- ment.

Credit risk Credit risk is the risk of economic loss arising from a counter- partys failure to repay or service debt in accordance with the contractual terms.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Credit risk encompasses both the direct risk of default and the risk of a deterioration of creditworthi- ness as well as concentration risks.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The maximum risk positions of fnancial assets which are gen- erally subject to credit risk are equal to their carrying amounts at the balance sheet date (without consideration of collateral, if available).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
There is also a risk of default from irrevocable loan commitments which had not been utilized as of that date, as well as from fnancial guarantees.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The maximum risk posi- tion in these cases is equal to the expected future cash out- fows.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Table  D.87 shows the maximum risk positions at the reporting date.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Classify the following text:
Country risk Country risk is the risk of economic loss arising from changes of political, economic, legal or social conditions in the respec- tive country, e.g., resulting from sovereign measures such as expropriation or interdiction of foreign currency transfers.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group is exposed to country risk mainly resulting from investments in subsidiaries, associated compa- nies, joint ventures and joint operations, as well as from cross-border trade receivables.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Country risks also arise from cross-border cash deposits at fnancial institutions.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group manages these risks via country exposure limits (e.g., for hard currency portfolios of fnancial services entities).
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
An internal rating system serves as a basis for risk-oriented country exposure management; it assigns all countries to risk classes, with consideration of capital market indications of country risks.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Certain benchmark interest rates, including those of the Lon- don Interbank Ofer Rate (for US dollar, British pound sterling, Swiss franc and Japanese yen), have comprehensively reformed internationally by the end of 2022.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
As a result, those interest rates were gradually abolished and replaced with alter- native risk-free reference rates.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Alternative interest rates are being developed on a national level in the context of the respective legal systems and currencies; they can therefore vary with regards to their structure, methodology and period of publication.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In 2022, the majority of the transactions afected by the benchmark reform either expired, ended prematurely or switched to the risk-free reference rates.
Answer: Non-Environmental

The Daimler Truck Group expects that the remaining conversion of reference rates of hedging instruments and their underlying transactions to be identical and without any signifcant delay in time.

Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
As part of its risk management system, the Daimler Truck Group employs value-at-risk analyses.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
In performing these analyses, Daimler quantifes its market risk caused by changes in foreign currency exchange rates and interest rates and cer- tain commodity prices on a regular basis by predicting the potential loss over a target time horizon (holding period) and confdence level.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The value-at-risk calculations are based on the following assumptions:  potential losses related to fair value changes, and  a 99% confdence level at a holding period of fve days.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Group calculates the value at risk for exchange rates according to the variance-covariance approach.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
When calculating value at risk using the variance-covariance approach, the Daimler Truck Group frst computes the current market value of the Groups fnancial instruments portfolio.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Then, the sensitivity of the portfolio value to changes in the rel- evant market risk factors, such as particular foreign currency exchange rates of specifc maturities, is quantifed.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Based on volatilities and correlations of these market risk factors, which are obtained from the RISKMETRICSTM dataset, a statistical dis- tribution of potential changes in the portfolio value at the end of the holding period is computed.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The loss which is reached or exceeded with a probability of only1% can be derived from this calculation and represents the value at risk.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The Daimler Truck Groups overall currency exposure is reduced by natural hedging, which consists of the currency exposures of the business operations of diferent entities and segments partially ofsetting each other at a Group level.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
These natural hedges eliminate the need for hedging to the extent of the matched exposures.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:

Daimler Truck AGs targeted hedge ratios for forecasted oper- ating cash fows in foreign currency are indicated by a refer- ence model.

Classify the following text:
On the one hand, the hedging horizon is naturally limited by uncertainty related to cash fows that lie far in the future; on the other hand, it may also be limited by the fact that appropriate currency contracts are not available.

Classify the following text:
This ref- erence model aims to limit risks for the Daimler Truck Group from unfavorable movements in exchange rates while preserv- ing some fexibility to participate in favorable developments.

The risk for the consolidated financial statements Receivables from fnancial services (EUR 22,971 million) result- ing from the fnancing and leasing activities of the Daimler Truck Group include receivables from sales fnancing with cus- tomers, receivables from sales fnancing with dealers and receivables from fnance lease contracts.

Classify the following text:
The loss allowances on these receivables amounts at the reporting date to EUR 546 million.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The calculation of the loss allowances is based on expected credit losses and therefore also includes expectations regard- ing the future.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
The expected credit losses are recognised by means of a three-parameter procedure for the determination of loss allowances.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
At the same time, various factors determin- ing the value, such as the determination of statistical default probabilities and loss rates, the possible amount receivable on default, the parameter transfer criteria that are related to a signifcant change in the default risk of borrowers, and the cal- culation of future cash fows are taken into account.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
Further- more, macroeconomic scenarios (basis scenarios, optimistic and pessimistic scenarios), the identifcation of which to a high degree includes discretionary judgements and uncertainties, fow into the calculation.
Answer: Non-Environmental

Classify the following text:
